qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: GroovyShell.evaluate() code injection via unsanitized matrix filter values in .jervis.yml
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable code and root cause: the finding concerns unescaped matrix filter values reaching GroovyShell.evaluate() in LifecycleGenerator.groovy and MultiPlatformGenerator.groovy, while the advisory concerns deterministic PBKDF2 salt derivation from sha256Sum(passphrase) in SecurityIO.groovy.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory is CVE-2025-68703/GHSA-36h5-vrq6-pp34 for Jervis SecurityIO.groovy deriving PBKDF2 salt from sha256Sum(passphrase), causing deterministic derived keys and precomputation exposure. The only final qualifying finding reports a separate Groovy expression injection path through matrix filter construction and GroovyShell.evaluate().
evidence: Advisory details cite SecurityIO.groovy lines 869-870 and 894-895, where salt is derived from sha256Sum(passphrase), with a patch to generate and store a random salt per password.

groovyshell_code_injection_via_matrix_filter/vulnerability_report.json cites LifecycleGenerator.groovy:561, :564, :571 and MultiPlatformGenerator.groovy:526 as the source-to-sink path for GroovyShell.evaluate() code injection.
Session workflow metadata lists groovyshell_code_injection_via_matrix_filter as the only final vulnerability folder.
